tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low urine specific gravity, and low HbA1c. These findings suggest impaired renal function, likely nephrotic-range proteinuria, and possible tubular dysfunction. The presence of glucosuria with normal blood glucose and low HbA1c may indicate renal glycosuria. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
